_meta.description: Documentation frame metadata extracted from carbon Figma file. Feeds Cluster 3 dimension 3.5.

components.pageDescription:
While this process can be tedious and time consuming, this guide takes careful consideration to minimize breaking changes and unwanted overrides in components and files with multiple layers of dependencies. To watch a demo of this process, watch the Figma Friday playback.

Migrate libraries with fewer dependencies first.
Create a temporary duplicate library for testing.
Customize a complex component as a breaking-change reference point for migration.
Open the Libraries panel, and swap to the new v11 All Themes library.
Note any unmatched components/styles for troubleshooting.
Swap libraries and wait for the update to complete.
Consider breaking up large libraries if needed and reconsolidating after the migration.
Repeat the process for each Carbon theme used.
Apply inline theming for components with mixed themes.
Set the desired theme in the Page section of the right panel.
Check for unintended overrides and resolve issues.
Repeat for every page in the file.
Perform the process on the main file for confident updates.
Test the migration process thoroughly.
Publish the fully migrated library and communicate changes.
Open working/hand-off files and accept updates from the library.
Swap libraries in each working/hand-off file.
Verify all files are now completely migrated.

The v11 Figma update is available.
Teams will need to swap out assets from v10 to v11 assets. The v10 Figma files will not receive a continuous update and will remain permanently on v10.
We recommend teams that are moving from v10 to v11 to start new files from scratch using the “(v11) All themes - Carbon Design System” library (previously referred to as “(v11) White theme - Carbon Design System”). Learn more in  Deprecation of theme files.
For information about migration and what is available in our v11 release, visit the Design migration guide.
Effects currently can not use variable modes. However, this feature will be released in a future Figma update.

components.pageDescription:
Example steps for using the LG 1056px breakpoint with modals. 
Frame — Create a frame and select it.
— Set the Fill to the $overlay color style.
— Set the variable viewport in the width “W” of the frame to “Breakpoint” > “Viewport size”. — Add auto layout to the frame and align to center.
— With the frame selected, in the “Layer” section, click the “Variables” icon. Navigate to the “Breakpoint” from the menu and select the “LG - XL (1056px - 1312px) option.

Modal component — Place the modal component within the frame.
— Change the width “W” to “Fill”.
— Then you can change the size of the modal from the right panel and it will respond appropriately in width.
The primary goal of this product or file
